title: Quant (QNT) Price Forecast & Scenarios
content: Analyzing the price trajectory of Quant (QNT) requires a close look at historical performance data and current market sentiment. Over the past 30 days, Quant has experienced a range between $61.85 and $75.03, reflecting a period of relative consolidation with a 30-day change of -4.77%. On a 1-year horizon, the token has fluctuated from a low of $56.85 to a high of $135.00, demonstrating that while the current price of {{LIVE_PRICE}} sits well below its all-time high of $427.42, historical volatility remains a defining feature. | Metric | Details | | :--- | :--- | | Current Price | {{LIVE_PRICE}} | | 30d Range | $61.85 - $75.03 | | 1y Range | $56.85 - $135.00 | | 1y Change | -14.97% | | Market Cap | {{LIVE_MARKET_CAP}} | ## Technical Analysis of Support and Resistance Historically, the support level near the 1-year low of $56.85 has served as a crucial defensive line. Should price action break below this, psychological support may be tested at lower levels. Conversely, resistance is clustered near the $135.00 mark, which represented the upper bound of the last 12-month cycle. Breaking these levels would require a significant shift in market volume, which currently stands at $7,343,650 over the last 24 hours. ## Comparative Market Context When evaluating QNT against infrastructure peers like **Chainlink** and **The Graph**, it is clear that Quant's narrative strength is tied to its unique enterprise focus. While those peers dominate in data availability and oracle services, Quant's specialized focus on interoperability creates a different supply-demand profile. Investors often monitor how these assets correlate during neutral market phases to gauge capital rotation. ## Scenarios for Market Cap Development Given the Risk Score of 8/10, price scenarios must be weighed against macroeconomic factors. In a **bearish scenario**, if market liquidity dries up, prices may drift toward the historical lows seen in the past year. In a **base case**, stabilization around current levels could persist as the project matures. In a **bullish scenario**, increased institutional adoption of Overledger could catalyze upward momentum, though this is heavily dependent on broad market conditions and sector-specific performance. ## Risk Factors and Volatility With a volatility index of 23, Quant is susceptible to sharp moves on low volume. Investors should consider that the token is currently trading at a significant discount from its all-time high, with an 83.56% decline from its 2021 peak. This disparity necessitates a cautious approach to risk management, as the project faces intense pressure from both established rivals and emerging L2 solutions. ## FAQ - How do I interpret the 30-day price change? The -4.77% change in the last 30 days suggests a trend of consolidation within the $61.85 to $75.03 range, typical of a neutral market phase. - What are the key resistance levels for QNT? Based on 1-year data, $135.00 serves as a primary resistance zone, while $56.85 acts as a long-term historical floor. - Is Quant's market cap currently growing? With a market cap of {{LIVE_MARKET_CAP}}, Quant is ranked {{LIVE_RANK}}, placing it firmly in the mid-cap category, where price sensitivity to market news is typically heightened. - How does QNT compare to its competitors? QNT is positioned differently than The Graph or Chainlink, focusing on enterprise-grade operating systems rather than oracle or indexing services. --- *Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. Always do your own research (DYOR).*
